criteria.name: keyboard_operable_studio_controls
criteria.description: Every interactive control — rail entries, header selects, accordion headers, form fields, verdict toggles, case include toggles, history entries, Undo, Redo, Export, Import, and copy controls — is reachable and operable with the keyboard alone (Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter/Space), each showing a visible focus indicator when focused.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Keyboard focus reached the controls, but several buttons showed transparent focus outlines. Evidence: keyboard_operable_studio_controls-shared.png

criteria.name: criterion_form_focus_trap_and_return
criteria.description: The criterion form dialog, Export center, and Import surface trap focus while open, close on Escape, and return focus to the control that opened them.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Focus stayed within the criterion dialog, but Escape did not close it when focus was inside a form field. Evidence: criterion_form_focus_trap_and_return-shared.png

criteria.name: icons_have_accessible_names
criteria.description: Meaningful icons (chevrons, copy, close, load-bearing badges) expose descriptive accessible names or alt text so assistive technology can identify them.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Several standalone SVG icons had no accessible name or alt text. Evidence: keyboard_operable_studio_controls-shared.png

criteria.name: validation_and_toasts_announced
criteria.description: Form validation messages and confirmation toasts are conveyed visually and via association or an ARIA live region so assistive technology can announce them.
criteria.reasoning: Validation used alert nodes and the Copy confirmation appeared in both polite status and assertive alert live regions. Evidence: validation_and_toasts_announced-shared.png

criteria.name: criterion_form_fields_explicitly_labeled
criteria.description: Criterion form fields (id, name, description, type, weight, importance, likert min/max, version) use explicit label elements associated with their controls.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Criterion fields displayed nearby text, but the controls were not associated with explicit label elements. Evidence: criterion_form_focus_trap_and_return-shared.png

criteria.name: accordion_and_disclosure_expose_expanded_state
criteria.description: Accordion headers and the Rationale notes disclosure expose their expanded or collapsed state to assistive technology (for example aria-expanded).
criteria.reasoning: Accordion rows and Rationale notes buttons exposed aria-expanded and updated it on activation. Evidence: accordion_and_disclosure_expose_expanded_state-shared.png

criteria.name: studio_landmarks_present
criteria.description: The app exposes semantic landmarks (for example nav/complementary for the rail and main for the canvas) so assistive technology can navigate the studio.
criteria.reasoning: The app exposed complementary rail, navigation, main canvas, regions, and version-history landmarks. Evidence: keyboard_operable_studio_controls-shared.png

criteria.name: text_and_controls_have_contrast
criteria.description: Rubric name, criterion names, type/importance chips, validation copy, and primary controls meet sufficient contrast against their surfaces.
criteria.reasoning: Rubric headings, criterion names, chips, validation text, and primary controls were visibly readable; measured primary text contrast was sufficient. Evidence: keyboard_operable_studio_controls-shared.png

criteria.name: semantic_roles_for_interactive_chrome
criteria.description: Rail entries, accordion headers, selects, toggles, and dialogs use semantic HTML or equivalent ARIA roles (button, dialog, listbox) rather than unlabeled clickable text alone.
criteria.reasoning: Interactive chrome used buttons, native/select comboboxes, dialog roles, tabs, and switch-role checkboxes. Evidence: semantic_roles_for_interactive_chrome-shared.png

criteria.name: reduced_motion_respected
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion set, accordion, Tune-bar, aggregate, dialog, and toast animations are removed and state changes apply instantly while every flow remains available.
criteria.reasoning: In the reduced-motion browser, prefers-reduced-motion matched, dialog transitions were effectively 1 microsecond, and accordion/dialog state changes applied immediately. Evidence: reduced_motion_respected-shared.png

criteria.name: multi_facet_reload_resets_to_seed
criteria.description: Starting from seed: add a valid criterion, switch to Tune and exclude one labelled case, flip a preview verdict, and change aggregation mode; then reload. Confirm every facet coherently resets to the seeded baseline — exactly 4 rubrics at seeded versions, Response Quality open, seeded history present, and no surviving custom criterion or toggled case — never a mix of surviving and reverted facets.
criteria.reasoning: Reload restored 6 seeded criteria, v2.1.0, and the seeded baseline after custom edits, tune exclusions, threshold changes, and aggregation changes. Evidence: multi_facet_reload_resets_to_seed-shared.png

criteria.name: aggregation_mode_reversal_proves_live_state
criteria.description: Selection-reversal proof (genre stand-in for sort reversal): in the sample submission panel set a mixed pass/fail pattern, note the weighted-mean aggregate, switch aggregation mode to all-pass and note the different aggregate (0 percent when any fail), then switch back to weighted mean. Confirm the original weighted-mean aggregate returns — proving the score is derived from live verdicts and mode, not two hardcoded snapshots.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Weighted mean produced 57% initially and verdict chips changed results, but the required mixed-verdict → all-pass 0% → weighted-mean return sequence was not fully observed. Evidence: different_verdicts_change_aggregate-shared.png

criteria.name: tune_derived_view_responds_to_includes
criteria.description: Derived-view sensitivity: in Tune, note precision/recall/F1 and the summary strip, exclude two labelled cases, then change a likert pass threshold so seeded case scores straddle it. Confirm bars and summary change meaningfully for each input rather than redrawing identically regardless of toggles/threshold.
criteria.reasoning: Excluding case-01 and case-02 changed inclusion from 12/12 to 10/12 and changed precision, recall, F1, confusion values, and the clarity threshold result. Evidence: tune_derived_view_responds_to_includes-shared.png

criteria.name: criterion_edit_echoes_across_surfaces
criteria.description: Cross-view echo: after a version-gated save that changes a criterion name or weight, without reload confirm the accordion row, header rollup (criteria count / load-bearing count / weight sum), Tune bars for that criterion, preview panel row, and Export center structured-text / rubric-json / package-json previews all reflect the same updated values.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The edited criterion name was observed in the criteria row, version rollup, Tune content, and export JSON, but the preview row was not directly verified in the same completed workflow. Evidence: criterion_edit_echoes_across_surfaces-shared.png

criteria.name: add_criterion_count_delta_exact
criteria.description: Count-delta integrity: measure the header criteria count, submit one valid Add criterion form, and re-measure immediately — the count increases by exactly one (no off-by-one, no lagging counter), and exactly one new accordion row appears.
criteria.reasoning: Adding one valid criterion changed the header from 6 to 7 and produced exactly one new accordion row, session-test. Evidence: add_criterion_count_delta_exact-shared.png

criteria.name: different_verdicts_change_aggregate
criteria.description: Input-dependent output: with aggregation mode set to weighted mean, apply the All fail chip and note the aggregate, then apply the All pass chip. Confirm the two outcomes differ (0 percent vs 100 percent class outcomes) rather than byte-identical aggregates.
criteria.reasoning: All fail produced 0% under weighted mean and All pass produced 100%, directly demonstrating input-dependent aggregate output. Evidence: different_verdicts_change_aggregate-shared.png

criteria.name: interleaved_add_and_tune_flows
criteria.description: Interleaved-flow integrity: open Add criterion and fill some fields without submitting, switch to Tune and toggle one case include, then return and finish submitting a valid new criterion; confirm exactly one new row appears, the Tune include toggle persisted for that session, and neither flow corrupted the other's state.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: The interleaved unsaved Add form workflow was not completed and verified through the visible UI. Evidence: add_criterion_count_delta_exact-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_then_repopulate_criteria
criteria.description: Edge-state round-trip: delete every criterion (each with major bump) until the criteria empty state appears and Tune/preview show empty states, then add two valid criteria with required bumps; confirm empty states clear, both rows appear, and header rollup tracks through both transitions.
criteria.reasoning: All six criteria were deleted with major bumps; the criteria count reached 0, Tune showed no criteria to tune, Preview showed no verdicts to preview, and two valid criteria repopulated the studio with count 2. Evidence: empty_then_repopulate_criteria-shared.png

criteria.name: export_pipeline_contains_session_work
criteria.description: Full-pipeline end state: add a valid CriterionUpsert, open Export center, confirm structured text plus rubric JSON (schemaVersion rubric-document-v1) and package JSON (schemaVersion rubric-package-v1, library Rubric Studio) include the new criterion id/name; delete that criterion with a major bump and confirm the next export omits it — proving artifacts compile live from the store.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Export was directly observed containing a newly added criterion and live schema fields, but the same criterion's completed delete-then-export omission check was not fully verified. Evidence: export_pipeline_contains_session_work-shared.png

criteria.name: import_export_round_trip_reconstructs
criteria.description: Export a RubricPackage after a create, note rubrics length and a criterion id, diverge the studio, Import the package, and confirm rail counts, opened criteria, and a fresh export reconstruct to match the imported document without a reload.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: The package import, divergence, import reconstruction, and fresh-export comparison workflow was not completed in the live browser. Evidence: export_pipeline_contains_session_work-shared.png

criteria.name: undo_round_trip_restores_prior_state
criteria.description: Undo round-trip: add a valid criterion (or flip a preview verdict), Undo once and confirm the prior criteria count or aggregate returns on every surface that showed the mutation, then Redo and confirm the mutation returns identically.
criteria.reasoning: Undo restored the prior criterion name, and redo restored the edited name and threshold; the mutation was observed returning on the Tune surface. Evidence: undo_round_trip_restores_prior_state-shared.png

criteria.name: seeded_rubric_rail_walkthrough
criteria.description: On first load the left rail lists exactly 4 seeded rubric files — Response Quality, Code Review Depth, Safety Screening, and Summarization Fidelity — each entry showing the rubric name, a semantic-version badge (three dot-separated numbers), and a criteria count; the default open rubric (Response Quality) shows at least 6 criteria and each other rubric, when opened, shows at least 4
criteria.reasoning: The initial browser view showed exactly four seeded rubrics with names, semantic versions, and counts; Response Quality had 6 criteria and the others had 4–5. Evidence: seeded_rubric_rail_walkthrough-shared.png

criteria.name: header_card_selects_and_rollup
criteria.description: The opened rubric's header card shows the rubric name, its current semantic version, an arbiter model select offering exactly quartz-arbiter-2, sable-jury-9, and cinder-panel-1, an aggregation mode select offering exactly weighted mean, required-pass, and all-pass, and a derived rollup line with total criteria count, load-bearing count (weight 3 or more), and weight sum; changing either select updates the header without a page reload
criteria.reasoning: The header visibly showed both selects, rollup metrics, and live changes to arbiter model and aggregation mode without reload. Evidence: header_card_selects_and_rollup-updated.png

criteria.name: criterion_accordion_anatomy
criteria.description: Each collapsed criterion row shows an id, a name, a type badge reading binary or likert with min and max values, a weight value, and an importance label (must have or nice to have); expanding the row with a click reveals the full multiline description and a Rationale notes disclosure that expands on activation and stays open for that criterion while the app remains open
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Criterion rows and expanded descriptions were observed, but the required rationale disclosure staying open was not completed and verified. Evidence: criterion_accordion_anatomy-shared.png

criteria.name: load_bearing_accent_on_heavy_weights
criteria.description: Every criterion whose weight is 3 or higher renders with a distinct load-bearing accent treatment (accent border or badge) that criteria with lower weights visibly lack, and editing a criterion's weight across the 3 boundary adds or removes the accent accordingly
criteria.reasoning: Heavy criteria visibly used the distinct load-bearing treatment while lower-weight criteria did not. Evidence: header_card_selects_and_rollup-shared.png

criteria.name: add_criterion_count_delta
criteria.description: Submitting the Add criterion form with a valid CriterionUpsert payload (unique id matching lowercase letters/digits/hyphens, name 1-80, description 1-2000, type binary or likert with likertMin/likertMax when likert, weight 0.5-5 in 0.5 steps, importance must-have or nice-to-have) appends exactly one new accordion row showing those values and increases the header criteria count by exactly one, measured immediately before and after the submit
criteria.reasoning: A valid added criterion appeared as a new row and the header/rail count increased from 6 to 7. Evidence: add_criterion_count_delta-after.png

criteria.name: criterion_form_inline_validation
criteria.description: Submitting the criterion form with an empty name or description, a duplicate id, an id that violates the allowed pattern, a weight outside 0.5-5, an importance outside must-have|nice-to-have, or a likert range where min is not less than max shows inline per-field messages naming the offending fields, keeps the submit control disabled until all required fields are valid, and adds no criterion (the criteria count is unchanged)
criteria.reasoning: The Add criterion dialog immediately showed per-field required messages and a disabled submit control for empty fields. Evidence: add_criterion_count_delta-after.png

criteria.name: minor_bump_gate_on_description_edit
criteria.description: After editing a criterion's description or type, the save control is disabled and an inline explanation states that at least a minor version bump is required; entering a version with the middle number increased (and third number reset) enables save, and saving applies the edit
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The description edit opened a minor-bump gate with save disabled, but the correct minor bump and successful save were not completed. Evidence: wrong_bump_names_violation_kind-shared.png

criteria.name: wrong_bump_names_violation_kind
criteria.description: Attempting to save a description/type change with only a patch bump shows a violation message containing the phrase minor bump required, and attempting to save a criterion deletion with only a minor or patch bump shows major bump required; in both cases the rubric, its version badge, and its history remain unchanged
criteria.reasoning: A description change with a patch bump displayed “minor bump required,” and deletion displayed “major bump required,” while the document remained unchanged. Evidence: wrong_bump_names_violation_kind-shared.png

criteria.name: delete_requires_major_bump
criteria.description: Deleting a criterion asks for confirmation, then blocks save with an inline major-bump explanation until the version's first number is increased with the others reset; after a correct major bump and save, the criteria count decreases by exactly one and the rail badge shows the new major version
criteria.reasoning: Deletion confirmation, disabled save for an invalid bump, valid 3.0.0 major bump, count decrease, updated rail badge, and successful deletion were all observed. Evidence: delete_requires_major_bump-after.png

criteria.name: save_appends_history_entry
criteria.description: Saving a correctly bumped change appends exactly one new entry at the top of the version history panel showing the new version number, a timestamp, and a one-line summary naming the changed criterion, and the rail entry's version badge updates to match — all without a reload
criteria.reasoning: The completed deletion added a newest v3.0.0 history entry with timestamp and criterion summary, and updated the rail badge without reload. Evidence: delete_requires_major_bump-after.png

criteria.name: history_entry_opens_diff_view
criteria.description: Selecting a version history entry opens a read-only diff-style view where added criteria appear in a green-tinted added treatment, removed criteria in a red-tinted treatment with struck-through text, and changed criteria in an amber-tinted treatment showing before and after values; a Close or Back control returns to the editable criteria list
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: A changed-entry diff view opened and showed before/after values, but added and removed visual treatments were not fully verified. Evidence: history_entry_opens_diff_view-shared.png

criteria.name: tune_toggle_recomputes_metrics
criteria.description: Scripted probe: in the Tune view, record one criterion's precision, recall, and F1 numbers, toggle two labelled cases off and confirm the bars and numbers change along with the summary strip's included-case count and macro averages, then toggle them back on and confirm the original values are restored exactly; additionally, changing a likert criterion's pass threshold recomputes that criterion's bars to different values when seeded case scores straddle the threshold
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Toggling two labelled cases changed included count and macro metrics and restoring returned the original values, but threshold recomputation was not verified. Evidence: tune_toggle_recomputes_metrics-shared.png

criteria.name: aggregate_honors_mode_and_verdicts
criteria.description: Scripted probe: in the sample submission panel set a mix of pass and fail verdicts and record the weighted-mean aggregate; switch the aggregation mode to all-pass and confirm the aggregate drops to 0 percent; apply the All pass suggestion chip and confirm the aggregate reads 100 percent; flip a single verdict off under weighted mean and confirm the aggregate decreases by that criterion's weight share
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: All-pass mode visibly changed the aggregate from 0% to 100%, but the weighted-mean mix and single-verdict weight-share probe were not completed. Evidence: aggregate_honors_mode_and_verdicts-all-pass.png

criteria.name: export_center_reflects_live_rubric
criteria.description: Export center opens with three live-derived format tabs (structured text, rubric JSON, package JSON); after adding a new criterion, re-opening export includes that criterion's id and name in all three previews; Copy places exactly the displayed text on the clipboard with visible confirmation, and Download triggers a real file download

criteria.name: rubric_document_json_field_contract
criteria.description: The rubric JSON preview visibly includes schemaVersion exactly rubric-document-v1 plus name, version, arbiterModel (one of quartz-arbiter-2, sable-jury-9, cinder-panel-1), aggregationMode (one of weighted-mean, required-pass, all-pass), and a criteria array whose objects carry CriterionUpsert fields (id, name, description, type, weight, importance, and likertMin/likertMax when type is likert)
criteria.reasoning: The visible Rubric JSON preview contained rubric-document-v1, name, version, arbiterModel, aggregationMode, criteria, and CriterionUpsert fields including likert bounds. Evidence: rubric_document_json_field_contract-visible.png

criteria.name: empty_name_description_inline_before_submit

criteria.name: validation_names_field_not_bare_invalid

criteria.name: tune_and_aggregate_recompute_under_100ms
criteria.description: Toggling a labelled-case include or a preview verdict recomputes Tune bars or the aggregate score with UI response in under 100ms (perceived immediate).
criteria.reasoning: Case inclusion and verdict toggles updated counts and aggregate immediately during interaction. Evidence: rapid_verdict_toggling_never_hangs-shared.png

criteria.name: shell_visible_while_settling
criteria.description: While the app settles after first paint, the left rail and main canvas remain visible rather than a blank white screen with no controls.
criteria.reasoning: Fresh desktop load showed both the rubric library rail and main canvas present with meaningful UI. Evidence: shell_visible_while_settling-shared.png

criteria.name: seeded_criteria_list_scrolls_without_lag
criteria.description: With the default Response Quality rubric open (at least 6 criteria) and at least 10 labelled cases in Tune, scrolling the criteria list and case list shows no perceived lag.
criteria.reasoning: Tune view rendered 12 labelled cases and 6 criteria with responsive interaction and no observed lag. Evidence: seeded_criteria_list_scrolls_without_lag-shared.png

criteria.name: ui_interactive_during_recompute
criteria.description: During Tune-bar width easing or aggregate score animation, case toggles, verdict toggles, and header selects remain interactive — the UI does not freeze until transitions finish.
criteria.reasoning: Case switches and repeated verdict controls remained interactive while metrics and aggregate values updated. Evidence: rapid_verdict_toggling_never_hangs-shared.png

criteria.name: tune_bar_animation_holds_stable_frame_rate
criteria.description: Driven through real case toggles or threshold moves, Tune bar width easing maintains a smooth frame rate without visible hitching.
criteria.reasoning: Real case toggling updated Tune metrics; bar elements exposed a 0.5s width easing transition and no hitch was observed. Evidence: tune_bar_animation_holds_stable_frame_rate-shared.png

criteria.name: rapid_verdict_toggling_never_hangs
criteria.description: Rapid repeated verdict toggling (at least five flips in quick succession, including All pass / All fail chips) keeps the UI responsive with no hangs and leaves toggles and aggregate in agreement.
criteria.reasoning: Performed six rapid verdict/chip toggles; the UI remained responsive and left switches and the aggregate score in agreement. Evidence: rapid_verdict_toggling_never_hangs-shared.png

criteria.name: extended_session_stays_stable
criteria.description: An extended session of opening all 4 rubrics, switching Tune/preview, and exporting repeatedly shows no runaway memory growth or progressive slowdown that freezes the UI.
criteria.reasoning: Opened all 4 rubrics, switched Tune/Preview repeatedly, and exported all formats multiple times without freezing or progressive slowdown. Evidence: export_panel_stays_responsive-shared.png

criteria.name: export_panel_stays_responsive
criteria.description: Opening Export center and switching among structured text, rubric JSON, and package JSON keeps the studio interactive; the monospaced previews appear without a multi-second blank hang, and Copy/Download remain responsive.
criteria.reasoning: Export center displayed structured text, rubric JSON, and package JSON previews with character counts plus responsive Copy and Download controls. Evidence: export_panel_stays_responsive-shared.png

criteria.name: layout_adapts_1440_to_375
criteria.description: Resizing from 1440px desktop to 375px mobile keeps a usable rubric studio: rail (or its toggle), header card, and criteria/Tune/preview canvas remain present and operable at both widths.
criteria.reasoning: Desktop rail/canvas and mobile toggle/header/canvas are present and usable. Evidence: layout_adapts_1440_to_375-desktop.png; layout_adapts_1440_to_375-mobile.png

criteria.name: mobile_tap_targets_adequate
criteria.description: At 375px width, rail toggle, accordion headers, Add criterion, verdict toggles, case include toggles, history entries, Undo, Redo, Export, Import, and copy present tap targets at least 44px in at least one dimension.
criteria.reasoning: Measured mobile controls are at least 44px in one dimension; accordion rows are 100px high, verdict switches 52×48px, and header actions 44px high. Evidence: mobile_controls_tappable-open-rail.png

criteria.name: typography_readable_both_widths
criteria.description: Rubric name, section headings, criterion names, and metadata remain legible at both 1440px and 375px — type scales or reflows without becoming unreadably small on mobile.
criteria.reasoning: Rubric and criterion headings remain legible; mobile criterion names measured at 13.76px and rubric heading at 24.8px. Evidence: layout_adapts_1440_to_375-desktop.png; layout_adapts_1440_to_375-mobile.png

criteria.name: no_clip_or_overflow_at_375
criteria.description: At 375px width, no content clips or overflows the viewport; the Tune bars and diff view reflow to stacked layouts and Export center code blocks scroll within their own containers; Undo, Redo, Export, and Import remain fully visible and operable.
criteria.reasoning: At 375px, page scroll width measured 360–375px with no page-level overflow; content stacks and the Export dialog stays within the viewport. Evidence: fixed_chrome_remains_accessible-export-dialog.png

criteria.name: rail_collapses_at_1024
criteria.description: At widths of 1024px and below the left rail collapses behind a toggle control that opens it as an overlay; at desktop widths the rail is open by default.
criteria.reasoning: At 1024px the rail measured offscreen at x≈−286px while the open-library toggle remained visible; desktop screenshot shows the rail open. Evidence: rail_collapses_at_1024.png

criteria.name: narrow_stack_order_stays_usable
criteria.description: At 375px width, header card, criteria/Tune/preview content, and history reflow into a logical stacking order without covering each other into an unusable pile.
criteria.reasoning: At 375px the header, rubric content, view tabs, criteria/tune content, and history occupy sequential non-overlapping blocks. Evidence: tune_bars_and_code_block_reflow-tune-metrics.png

criteria.name: mobile_controls_tappable
criteria.description: At 375px width, tap/click activation works on the rail toggle, rail entries (when overlay open), accordion headers, case toggles, verdict toggles, and Export.
criteria.reasoning: Real clicks successfully opened the rail, selected a rail entry, expanded a criterion, toggled a verdict, switched views, and opened Export. Evidence: mobile_controls_tappable-open-rail.png; fixed_chrome_remains_accessible-export-dialog.png

criteria.name: no_horizontal_scroll_at_375
criteria.description: At 375px width the page does not produce page-level horizontal scrolling.
criteria.reasoning: Read-only measurement at 375px reported document scrollWidth no greater than the viewport client width. Evidence: fixed_chrome_remains_accessible-export-dialog.png

criteria.name: tune_bars_and_code_block_reflow
criteria.description: At 375px width, Tune precision/recall/F1 bars and the export monospaced code block size responsively — bars stack or shrink to fit, and the code block scrolls inside its container rather than forcing page overflow.
criteria.reasoning: Tune metrics reflow into a 2×2 mobile grid, case toggles remain visible, and Export code is contained in an overflow-auto code panel. Evidence: tune_bars_and_code_block_reflow-tune-metrics.png; fixed_chrome_remains_accessible-export-dialog.png

criteria.name: fixed_chrome_remains_accessible
criteria.description: Fixed or sticky studio chrome (rail toggle, header actions, Export) remains reachable at 375px and does not permanently obscure the criteria list or form dialogs.
criteria.reasoning: At 375px the header actions remain visible and the Export dialog is fully reachable, with close, format tabs, Copy, Download, and internally scrolling preview visible. Evidence: fixed_chrome_remains_accessible-export-dialog.png

criteria.name: shared_state_coherence
criteria.description: All surfaces derive from one shared collection: a saved criterion edit, add, or delete is immediately reflected in the accordion list, header rollups, rail badge and counts, Tune bars, preview panel, and Export center structured-text / rubric-json / package-json previews without a reload, and no surface shows stale data another surface already updated
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Adding a criterion updated the rail, header totals, list, and package export, but Tune bars and preview were not re-verified after the mutation. Evidence: session_add.png

criteria.name: no_storage_reload_seeded
criteria.description: localStorage and sessionStorage remain empty after adding, editing, and deleting criteria, saving version bumps, toggling cases and verdicts, exporting, importing, and undoing, and a page reload returns the app to its seeded baseline
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Storage was observed empty and reload restored the seeded six-criterion baseline, but the full edit/delete/version-bump/undo workflow was not completed. Evidence: reload_seeded.png

criteria.name: console_clean_full_exercise
criteria.description: The console is free of errors, warnings, and unhandled promise rejections on load and stays clean through adding, editing, deleting, version-gated saves, diff viewing, tuning, preview toggling, export, import, and undo/redo
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Console showed zero errors and warnings during the exercised paths, but the complete required workflow including undo/redo and all mutation paths was not completed. Evidence: shared_initial.png

criteria.name: cold_load_interactive_2s
criteria.description: The app is interactive (rail entries and header selects respond) within 2 seconds of a local cold load
criteria.reasoning: The cold local load rendered the rail and header controls promptly and they were interactable. Evidence: shared_initial.png

criteria.name: rapid_input_stability
criteria.description: Under rapid repeated input (fast verdict toggling, quick case toggles, rapid rubric switching), the UI stays responsive with no hangs, and derived numbers and Export center previews settle to values consistent with the final input state
criteria.reasoning: Three rapid verdict toggles completed without hanging; the preview settled to the expected final verdict and aggregate score. Evidence: rapid_preview.png

criteria.name: keyboard_operability_focus
criteria.description: Every interactive control — rail entries, header selects, accordion headers, disclosures, form fields, verdict and case toggles, history entries, Undo, Redo, Export, Import, and copy controls — is reachable and operable with the keyboard alone (Tab/Shift+Tab, Enter/Space), showing a visible focus indicator at each stop
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Some controls were observed with keyboard focus, but every listed control was not independently verified for keyboard operation and focus indication. Evidence: validation_dialog.png

criteria.name: dialog_focus_trap_escape
criteria.description: The criterion form dialog, Export center, and Import surface trap focus while open, close on the Escape key, and return keyboard focus to the control that opened them; accordion headers and disclosures expose an expanded or collapsed state attribute to the accessibility tree
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Import closed on Escape and returned focus, but the criterion form remained open after Escape; therefore the required dialog behavior is not satisfied. Evidence: validation_dialog.png

criteria.name: labels_and_error_association
criteria.description: Every form field has a visible label, and each validation message (criterion, version bump, import) names the field it belongs to and is programmatically associated with that field
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Visible labels and required-field errors were present, but the observed Weight validation error lacked a verified aria-describedby association. Evidence: validation_dialog.png

criteria.name: api_shaped_validation_rejects_invalid_payloads
criteria.description: Invalid CriterionUpsert submissions (empty required fields, duplicate id, likert min not less than max, weight outside 0.5-5, importance outside must-have|nice-to-have) and non-conforming Import packages are rejected with visible per-field or package errors and leave the collection unchanged
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Required-field, duplicate-ID, out-of-range-weight, and empty-import validation were observed, but likert-range and invalid-importance cases were not completed. Evidence: validation_dialog.png

criteria.name: end_state_export_is_portable_persistence
criteria.description: After session mutations, Download or Copy of package JSON yields a RubricPackage that includes schemaVersion rubric-package-v1, library Rubric Studio, and the session's criteria; good-app persistence is this portable package plus MCP reads — browser storage stays unused
criteria.reasoning: Package JSON visibly contained schemaVersion rubric-package-v1, library Rubric Studio, and the session-added criterion. Evidence: session_add.png

criteria.name: only_fictional_arbiter_names_rendered
criteria.description: Across the arbiter selector, seeded rubric metadata, Tune view, preview, history, and export previews, model or arbiter names are limited to quartz-arbiter-2, sable-jury-9, and cinder-panel-1; no real product, company, or model name is rendered anywhere in the UI.
criteria.reasoning: Observed arbiter names were limited to quartz-arbiter-2, sable-jury-9, and cinder-panel-1 across the UI and package preview. Evidence: session_add.png

criteria.name: add_criterion_updates_all_surfaces
criteria.description: Create flow: opening Add criterion, submitting a valid form appends exactly one new accordion row, increases the header criteria count by one, and the new criterion appears in the Tune view and sample-submission panel without a page reload.
criteria.reasoning: Valid create added exactly one row, changed the count from 6 to 7, and the new criterion appeared in Criteria, Tune, and Preview without reload. Evidence: add_criterion_updates_all_surfaces-shared.png

criteria.name: invalid_add_shows_inline_validation
criteria.description: Create flow: submitting the criterion form with an empty name or description shows immediate inline messages naming those fields, adds no criterion, and leaves the header criteria count unchanged.
criteria.reasoning: Empty Add dialog showed inline ID, Name, and Description required messages; the Add button was disabled and the count stayed at 6. Evidence: invalid_add_shows_inline_validation-shared.png

criteria.name: version_gated_description_edit_end_to_end
criteria.description: Version-gated edit end to end: on the default Response Quality rubric, edit one criterion's description, observe save disabled with the minor-bump explanation, enter a version that only increases the patch number and observe the violation message naming minor bump required, then enter a correct minor bump and save — the rail badge shows the new version, exactly one new history entry appears at the top naming that criterion, and selecting it shows the description marked as changed with before and after values, all without a reload.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Edit dialog accepted a changed description and exposed version continuation, but the required minor-bump violation, successful save, new history entry, and diff verification were not completed. Evidence: criterion_form_and_diff_overlays-shared.png

criteria.name: delete_with_major_bump_end_to_end
criteria.description: Delete with major bump: delete a criterion, confirm, observe the major-bump explanation, enter a correct major bump and save — the criteria count decreases by exactly one, the rail badge shows the major version, and the new history entry's diff view shows that criterion in the red-tinted removed treatment with struck-through text.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: The complete delete/confirm/major-version/diff workflow was not exercised. Evidence: reload_recovers_to_seeded_studio-shared.png

criteria.name: criteria_tune_preview_switch_retains_rubric
criteria.description: View/mode switch: switching among criteria, Tune, and preview modes on the opened rubric changes the canvas without a reload and keeps the same active rubric, header selects, and criteria set.
criteria.reasoning: Criteria, Tune, and Preview were switched live; Response Quality, its version, and its criterion set remained active. Evidence: criteria_tune_preview_switch_retains_rubric-shared.png

criteria.name: last_delete_reveals_criteria_empty_state
criteria.description: Edge: after deleting all criteria from a rubric (each with its major bump), the criteria list shows a clear empty state naming what belongs there and offering Add criterion, and Tune and preview show their own empty states instead of broken bars.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Deleting all criteria and verifying the three empty states was not exercised. Evidence: reload_recovers_to_seeded_studio-shared.png

criteria.name: tune_case_toggles_update_bars_and_summary
criteria.description: Tune sensitivity: in the Tune view, note one criterion's F1 value, exclude two labelled cases and observe the precision/recall/F1 bars and summary strip change, then re-include them and observe the original values restored exactly.
criteria.reasoning: Excluding labelled cases 01 and 02 changed the clarity F1 from 0.74 to 0.67 and summary values; re-including both restored 12/12, Macro F1 0.60, and clarity F1 0.74 exactly. Evidence: tune_case_toggles_update_bars_and_summary-shared.png

criteria.name: rail_collapse_preserves_open_rubric
criteria.description: At widths of 1024px and below the left rail collapses behind a toggle; opening the overlay, selecting a different seeded rubric, and closing the overlay keeps that rubric open in the canvas with workflow continuity.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: At 1024px the rail remained open and the close control was outside the viewport, so the overlay-selection-continuity workflow could not complete. Evidence: rail_collapse_preserves_open_rubric-shared.png

criteria.name: criterion_form_and_diff_overlays
criteria.description: Overlays: the Add/Edit criterion form dialog opens for create/edit and closes via Cancel or Escape without changing the rubric; selecting a history entry opens the read-only diff view and Close/Back returns to the editable criteria list.
criteria.reasoning: Add/Edit criterion dialogs opened; Escape closed the edit form without saving, and a history entry opened a read-only diff with before/after descriptions. Evidence: criterion_form_and_diff_overlays-shared.png

criteria.name: reload_recovers_to_seeded_studio
criteria.description: Recovery: after editing views, toggles, or pending form state, a page reload returns the app to its seeded state — exactly 4 rubrics at seeded versions, Response Quality open by default, and all seeded history entries present — with no dead end requiring a second reload.
criteria.reasoning: Reload returned to Response Quality with 4 rubrics, seeded versions, 6 criteria, and all four seeded history entries. Evidence: reload_recovers_to_seeded_studio-shared.png

criteria.name: export_session_flow_to_artifact
criteria.description: Export session flow: add a valid criterion, open Export center, confirm structured text, rubric JSON (schemaVersion rubric-document-v1 with name/version/arbiterModel/aggregationMode/criteria), and package JSON (schemaVersion rubric-package-v1, library Rubric Studio, rubrics, generatedAt) include that criterion, then Download or Copy the active format.
criteria.reasoning: After creating a criterion, Structured text, rubric JSON with rubric-document-v1 fields, and package JSON with rubric-package-v1/library/rubrics/generatedAt visibly included it; Download produced rubric-package.json. Evidence: export_session_flow_to_artifact-shared.png

criteria.name: import_round_trip_flow
criteria.description: Import round-trip flow: export the package after a create, diverge the studio, Import the JSON, and confirm rail counts, opened criteria, and a fresh export reconstruct to match the imported package without a reload.
criteria.reasoning: After diverging to Code Review Depth, importing the downloaded package restored Response Quality with 7 criteria and seeded history, and a fresh export remained available with the imported criterion. Evidence: import_round_trip_flow-shared.png

criteria.name: undo_round_trip_flow
criteria.description: Undo round-trip: add a valid criterion or flip a preview verdict, Undo and confirm the prior criteria count or aggregate returns on accordion/header/Tune/preview, then Redo and confirm the mutation returns.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Add, Undo, and Redo correctly changed the header/list count between 7 and 8 and Tune/Preview showed the added criterion on redo, but the full required prior-state verification across all specified surfaces after Undo was not completed. Evidence: undo_round_trip_flow-shared.png

criteria.name: studio_regions_separated
criteria.description: At 1440px width the app composes as a fixed-width left rail, a main canvas with the header card above the criteria list, and a distinct version history surface, with the regions separated by hairline borders or elevation rather than running together, and spacing following a consistent rhythm across rail entries, criterion rows, history entries, and Tune bars with no crowded or orphaned regions
criteria.reasoning: Desktop view visibly separates the fixed rail, main canvas, criteria list, and version-history card with borders, cards, and consistent spacing. Evidence: studio_regions_separated-shared.png

criteria.name: dual_accent_discipline
criteria.description: One accent color consistently marks selection and primary actions while a second, visibly different hue marks load-bearing criteria (weight 3 or more) and the load-bearing rollup count; type badges (binary vs likert with range) and importance labels render as visually distinct chip treatments consistent across every criterion row; the two accents are distinguishable side by side and applied consistently across rail, list, Tune, and preview surfaces
criteria.reasoning: Selection/primary actions use purple, while load-bearing criteria and rollup use orange; type and importance badges are visibly distinct and consistent. Evidence: studio_regions_separated-shared.png

criteria.name: diff_color_language_consistent
criteria.description: In the diff view, added entries carry a green-tinted treatment, removed entries a red-tinted treatment with strikethrough, and changed entries an amber-tinted treatment with before and after values, each also carrying a non-color label or icon, and all text stays readable against its tint
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The live diff view showed only a Changed entry with before/after text; added green and removed red/strikethrough entries were not observed. Evidence: diff_color_language_consistent-shared.png

criteria.name: typography_hierarchy_clear
criteria.description: The rubric name is visibly larger than section headings, which are larger than criterion names, which are larger than body and metadata text, consistently across views; Export center previews render in a monospaced face distinct from the UI face
criteria.reasoning: The rubric title is visibly larger than section and criterion headings, with smaller metadata; the export preview is rendered in a distinct monospace style. Evidence: studio_regions_separated-shared.png; export_center_format_tabs_visible-shared.png

criteria.name: component_state_treatments
criteria.description: Buttons, selects, toggles, and form fields show distinct default, hover, focus (visible ring), disabled, and error treatments; the gated save control in its disabled state is visibly muted and sits adjacent to its inline explanation; Undo and Redo appear visibly disabled when their stacks are empty
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Disabled Undo/Redo and the muted gated save with inline explanation were observed, but complete distinct default, hover, focus, and error treatments were not directly demonstrated. Evidence: component_state_treatments-shared.png; component_state_treatments-focus-error-shared.png

criteria.name: export_center_format_tabs_visible
criteria.description: Export center presents format tabs for structured text, rubric JSON, and package JSON with a monospace code preview plus Copy and Download; Import sits as a clear companion control
criteria.reasoning: Export center visibly provides Structured text, Rubric JSON, and Package JSON tabs, a monospace live preview, Copy, Download, and companion Import controls. Evidence: export_center_format_tabs_visible-shared.png

criteria.name: responsive_reflow_clean
criteria.description: At 1024px the left rail collapses behind a toggle that opens it as an overlay, and at 375px width no content clips or overflows the viewport, no page-level horizontal scrolling appears, the Tune bars and diff view reflow to stacked layouts, and Export center code blocks scroll within their own containers
criteria.reasoning: At 1024px the rail collapses behind an Open rubric library toggle and appears as an overlay; at 375px content remains within the viewport, Tune and diff content stack, and the export code container has overflow-x:auto while body width remains 375px. Evidence: responsive_reflow_clean-1024.png; responsive_reflow_clean-375.png; responsive_reflow_clean-diff-375.png; responsive_reflow_clean-tune-375.png; responsive_reflow_clean-export-375.png

criteria.name: consistent_capitalization_studio_labels
criteria.description: Where the app renders headings and section titles (rubric name, Tune, Export, Import, Rationale notes, history summaries), they use one consistent capitalization convention throughout.
criteria.reasoning: Headings and section titles consistently use sentence-style capitalization, with uppercase reserved for eyebrow labels. Evidence: rationale-expanded.png

criteria.name: specific_verbs_on_actions
criteria.description: Where the app renders button or action labels, they use specific labels such as Add criterion, Export, Import, Undo, Redo, and Copy rather than generic Submit/OK when a specific label is possible.
criteria.reasoning: Observed specific action labels including Add criterion, Edit criterion, Delete, Undo, Redo, Import, Export, Copy, and Download. Evidence: export-modal.png

criteria.name: validation_names_problem_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders criterion-form, version-bump, or import validation messages, they name the field or violation kind (name, description, id, schemaVersion, library, minor bump required, major bump required) and the fix, not only a bare rejection like Invalid.
criteria.reasoning: Validation identifies specific fields and fixes, including “ID is required,” “Name is required,” “Description is required,” and “Package JSON is required; paste an exported package.” Evidence: criterion-add-form.png, import-view.png

criteria.name: empty_states_explain_next_step
criteria.description: Where the app renders empty states for an empty criteria list, empty Tune case set, or empty preview panel, the copy explains what belongs there and how to add or include content.
criteria.reasoning: No empty-state surfaces were rendered in the observed Criteria, Tune, or Preview views; those views contained their relevant content, so the conditional criterion passes vacuously. Evidence: criteria-initial.png, tune-view.png, preview-view.png

criteria.name: studio_copy_is_well_written
criteria.description: Where the app renders body copy (criterion descriptions, Rationale notes, history summaries, empty-state text), rate how free the text is of spelling and grammatical errors.
criteria.reasoning: Observed body copy is grammatical, clear, and professional across criteria guidance, rationale text, history summaries, and view descriptions. Evidence: rationale-expanded.png

criteria.name: terminology_consistent_across_surfaces
criteria.description: Where the app renders labels for the same concept in multiple places (criterion vs criteria, must have vs nice to have, weighted mean / required-pass / all-pass), terminology stays consistent rather than inventing conflicting names.
criteria.reasoning: The app consistently distinguishes criterion/criteria, must-have/nice-to-have, and weighted-mean terminology across the criteria, Tune, Preview, and history surfaces. Evidence: preview-view.png

criteria.name: version_and_metric_formatting_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders semantic versions, weights, and Tune precision/recall/F1 numbers, formatting is consistent across the rail badge, header, history entries, and bars (same decimal style and version shape).
criteria.reasoning: Versions consistently use three-part semantic forms such as v2.1.0; weights use decimal formatting, and Tune precision/recall/F1 values consistently use two decimals. Evidence: tune-view.png, criteria-initial.png

criteria.name: success_messages_are_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders confirmation feedback after save, delete, or copy, the copy states what happened rather than a vague affirmation alone.
criteria.reasoning: Copy feedback explicitly states “Copied to clipboard” and identifies the format as “Structured text.” Evidence: copy-feedback.png
